key_insight: Train PINNs on a coarse-to-fine sequence of collocation points, reusing learned weights at each stage to cut optimization cost while preserving accuracy.
engineering_relevance: This makes PINNs more practical for PDE simulation by reducing training time and improving scalability on harder, higher-resolution problems.
method: Start with a small coarse collocation set and train a single neural network.
Transfer the trained parameters to progressively finer collocation levels.
Use a scaling trick to reduce spectral bias for high-frequency PDEs.
evidence_signal: The method was tested on 2D/3D Poisson, convection-diffusion-reaction, and Helmholtz benchmarks, where it reportedly matched or exceeded conventional PINNs, SA-PINNs, and XPINNs with much less training time.
watch_item: Performance may depend on how the coarse-to-fine schedule and scaling are tuned, especially for stiff or strongly oscillatory PDEs.
paper_title: A new strategy for physics-informed neural networks based on hierarchical collocation point refinement
